definition: US semiconductor export controls operate as an institutional ratchet with 60-70% probability of further tightening vs <10% rollback. Three rounds of escalation (Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Jan 2025) demonstrate asymmetric institutional dynamics where tightening is costless politically while loosening requires multiple veto points. Bipartisan consensus makes de-escalation <15% regardless of election outcome.
evidence: Three rounds of escalation: Oct 2022, Oct 2023, Jan 2025
NSC, BIS, and congressional committees all favor tightening; no actor benefits from loosening
NVIDIA China revenue declined from ~25% to ~12-15%
Compound supply chain tax: 15-25% tariff + 5-10% rerouting + 3-5% compliance = 23-40% effective cost
China hawk is zero-cost politically; China dove is career-ending
open_questions: Whether further tightening targets foundry access (TSMC) or specific chip architectures
Impact of enforcement gaps and third-country circumvention on effective control
